sanskrit:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः॥
transliteration:
anta-kāle ca mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram
yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāsty atra saṁśayaḥ
literal_meaning: At the last moment, having remembered Me alone and abandoned the body, whoever departs attains My being—of this there is no doubt.
contextual_meaning: The final moment of life is a psychological ‘flash-bulb’ that freezes the dominant self-concept; whatever identity is most rehearsed becomes the post-death trajectory.
psychological_state: A calm, single-pointed focus that overrides the death-anxiety response; the psyche is rehearsing its own narrative ending.
trigger_scenario: A hospice patient reviewing life photos while listening to a mantra playlist, discovering which memories ‘light up’ the calmest affect.
anchor_text: Live every moment so your last thought is your highest self.
